prs.title: [Bugfix][ROCm][AITER MLA] Size persistent metadata buffers off max(max_num_seqs, max_cudagraph_capture_size)
prs.author: jin-amd
prs.body_head:
The AITER-MLA persistent scheduling buffers were sized off scheduler_config.max_num_seqs, but the kernel runs under cudagraphs at sizes up to compilation_config.max_cudagraph_capture_size. When the in-flight batch exceeds max_num_seqs,  the kernel walks past the end of the buffers (gibberish under fast_mode=True, GPU fault under fast_mode=False). Size off max(max_num_seqs, max_cudagraph_capture_size).



## Purpose

`AiterMLAMetadataBuilder.__init__` sizes the AITER persistent-MLA scheduling buffers (`work_meta_data`, `work_indptr`, `work_info_set`, `reduce_indptr`, `reduce_final_map`, `reduce_partial_map`, plus `paged_kv_*` / `qo_indptr` under full cudagraphs) off `scheduler_config.max_num_seqs`. But the kernel runs under cudagraph capture at batch sizes up to `compilation_config.max_cudagraph_capture_size`, which chunked prefill / mixed batches push above `max_num_seqs` (e.g. with `--max-num-seqs 16` the capture schedule is `[1,2,4,8,16,24,32]`, so `max_cudagraph_capture_size = 32`). Once the in-flight batch crosses that threshold the kernel walks past the end of the buffers — silent KV corruption (gibberish) under `fast_mode=True`, GPU memory access fault under `fast_mode=False`.

Sizing off `max(max_num_seqs, max_cudagraph_capture_size)` instead is a one-line change, cannot shrink any allocation, and grows the persistent metadata by ≤2× (a few MB of int32 indptrs per rank, dominated by `paged_kv_indices`).

Bug introduced by #36574. The same idiom is copied into open PRs #40889, #40871 and #29146, which will pick up the fix on rebase.

## Test Plan

Reproduce on DeepSeek-V3.2 TP=4 on MI300X with the smallest config that triggers the bug:

```bash
docker run -d --name dsv32-tp4-repro \
  --device /dev/dri --device /dev/kfd \
  --network host --ipc host --shm-size 32g \
  --group-add video --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
  -v /path/to/hf_cache:/root/.cache/huggingface/hub \
  -e VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=1 \
  --entrypoint vllm \
  vllm/vllm-

prs.title: [ROCm] [DeepSeekV4] Preliminary Enablement of DeepSeekV4 on ROCm
prs.author: tjtanaavllm
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

This PR supersedes https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/40871 as it is rebased against https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/40860 . (On CUDA side, PR https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/40760 supersedes PR https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/40871 )

## Test Plan

## Test Result

Server command:
```
max_num_seqs=64
max_num_batched_tokens=131072
tensor_parallel_size=4
export HF_HOME=/data/huggingface-cache
export VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=1

unset FLATMM_HIP_CLANG_PATH
MODEL=deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash
vllm serve ${MODEL} \
    --host localhost \
    --port 8000 \
    --dtype auto \
    --tensor-parallel-size ${tensor_parallel_size} \
    --max-num-seqs ${max_num_seqs} \
    --distributed-executor-backend mp \
    --trust-remote-code \
    --gpu-memory-utilization 0.40 \
    --moe-backend "triton_unfused" \
    --enforce-eager \
    --tokenizer-mode "deepseek_v4" \
    --async-scheduling

```

preliminary tests
lm_eval score
```
MODEL=deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash
lm_eval --model local-completions --model_args model=$MODEL,base_url=http://0.0.0.0:8000/v1/completions,num_concurrent=8,max_retries=10,max_gen_toks=2048 --batch_size auto --tasks gsm8k --num_fewshot 5 --limit 16  --output_path . 2>&1 | tee -a eval-oldforward.log
```

```
local-completions ({'model': 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash', 'base_url': 'http://0.0.0.0:8000/v1/completions', 'num_concurrent': 8, 'max_retries': 10, 'max_gen_toks': 2048}), gen_kwargs: ({}), limit: 16.0, num_fewshot: 5, batch_size: auto
|Tasks|Version|     Filter     |n-shot|  Metric   |   |Value|   |Stderr|
|-----|------:|----------------|-----:|-----------|---|----:|---|-----:|
|gsm8k|      3|flexible-extract|     5|exact_match|_  |0.875|_  |0.0854|
|     |       |strict-match    |     5|exact_match|_  |0.875|_  |0.0854|
```

prs.title: [ROCm][FEAT] AITER Fused Allreduce + RMSNorm
prs.author: vllmellm
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

This PR integrates AITER fused all-reduce + RMSNorm kernel for ROCm platforms, 

introduces:
-  `RocmAiterAllReduceFusionPass` that fuses the all-reduce collective with the subsequent RMSNorm (and fused-add-RMSNorm) 

AITER's `v0.1.10.post3` version exposes tw0 new function `custom_fused_ar_rms` and `custom_fused_ar_rms_quant` in `CustomAllReduce` class. In this PR we are only utilizing `custom_fused_ar_rms` from AITER's CustomAllReduce only during fusion pass pattern matching. 

###  Changes

in `vllm/_aiter_ops.py`:

- `AiterCustomAllreduceProto`: typed Protocol for the AITER `CustomAllreduce` object 
- `rocm_aiter_ops.initialize_aiter_allreduce(group, device)`: initializes the AITER all-reduce communicator.
- `rocm_aiter_ops.get_aiter_allreduce()` / `destroy_aiter_allreduce()`: lifecycle management
- `torch.ops.vllm.rocm_aiter_fused_allreduce_rmsnorm`: registered custom op with fake impl for `torch.compile`

in (`vllm/compilation/passes/pass_manager.py`):

When `fuse_allreduce_rms` is enabled and `rocm_aiter_ops.is_enabled()` is `True`, `RocmAiterAllReduceFusionPass` is used instead of `AllReduceFusionPass`. CUDA behavior is unchanged.

in (`vllm/config/vllm.py`):

On ROCm, `fuse_allreduce_rms` is now enabled automatically when:
- `VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=1`
- RMSNorm AITER ops are enabled
- `tensor_parallel_size > 1`

in `vllm/distributed/parallel_state.py`:

The AITER `CustomAllreduce.capture()` context manager is included alongside the existing `ca_comm.capture()` during CUDA graph capture, so the fused kernel is correctly captured.

## Tests

- `tests/compile/passes/distributed/test_fusion_all_reduce.py`: extended to test `RocmAiterAllReduceFusionPass` on ROCm; FP8/FP4 quant fusion tests are skipped on ROCm (not yet supported)
- `tests/compile/fusions_e2e/test_tp2_ar_rms.py`: `test_tp2_ar_rms_fusions` now runs on both CUDA and ROCm with ROCm attention backends (`ROCM_ATTN`, `ROCM_AITER_UNIFIED_ATTN`)
- `.buildk

prs.title: [Core][WIP] Check for GPU<->CPU sync during CI
prs.author: njhill
prs.body_head:
vLLM now uses asynchronous scheduling by default and in the majority of cases. Performance relies on the absence of any gpu<->cpu synchronizations on the main cuda stream, but such syncs can be opaque and it is easy for them to creep in accidentally.

This change adds a `VLLM_GPU_SYNC_CHECK` env var which enables `torch.cuda.set_sync_debug_mode` for the model forward pass and sampler, so that we can easily check for such syncs.

I'm trying first to enable it globally in the CI to flush out syncs that need to be fixed or where they are unavoidable and the check needs to be suppressed. Will then probably split the fixes into separate PR(s).

prs.title: Auto test target determination

## Purpose
This PR includes design and corresponding implementation of Automated test target determination using Claude Haiku

```

```
This runs against whatever origin/main...HEAD diff exists on the current branch and prints the comment body to stdout.
